findings.title: DELETE /api/tasks/:id — no explicit test for non-existent task → 404
findings.description: The route test suite has no explicit test case for DELETE /api/tasks/<non-existent-uuid> → 404. The implementation correctly returns 404 via AppError(404, 'NOT_FOUND', ...) in tasksService.remove().
findings.resolution: VERIFIED_NO_CODE_GAP — Code trace confirms AppError(404) → asyncHandler → errorHandler → 404 JSON response. All 39 tests pass. Cross-user DELETE test exercises identical findById→null→404 path.

findings.title: TOCTOU in service.remove() — repository return value discarded
findings.description: tasksService.remove() called findById (check) then remove (use) without checking remove's return value. If a concurrent request soft-deletes the task between check and use, remove() returns null but the service returned 204 No Content (success) instead of 404.
findings.resolution: FIXED — Changed remove() return type from Promise<void> to Promise<Task>. Now captures repository.remove() return value and throws AppError(404, 'NOT_FOUND', ...) if null. Mirrors existing pattern in service.update(). Two-line addition: const deleted = await tasksRepository.remove(id, userId); if (!deleted) throw new AppError(404, ...).

evidence.commit: src/services/tasks.service.ts — remove() now checks repository return value
evidence.diff: Lines 46-49: added const deleted = await ...; if (!deleted) throw ...

findings.title: Unreachable 403 ownership check in service.remove() and service.update()
findings.description: The ownership check (task.user_id !== userId → 403) is dead code. findById() already filters by user_id in SQL (WHERE user_id = $2), so task.user_id will always equal userId. Same issue in both service.remove() and service.update().
